payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You guys won't tell anyone, right? Anyway, on to the rest of the announcements! Next up, at E3, which is next week, we're going to be showing up on Thursday as part of GameSpot's E3 coverage. We're going to be doing cycling interviews with them starting at noon on Thursday, I believe, next week. So check in with GameSpot Thursday next week to have us sit in front of a camera and make complete and utter fools of ourselves, unlike we do here every Thursday. So that'll be a lot of fun. A reminder, me and Marisha are going to be at the Haven Expo convention in Australia beginning of July, so all you Australian folks in the Southern Hemisphere want to come and say hi, we'd love to see you. Comic Con, San Diego Comic Con is coming up.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And with a $3 discount, if you multiply that by 12, that's like getting a free Crate every year. I'm just making that up.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm not good at math so I believe you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I know, I was like, sounds legit. Sure. I'd believe that!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, if you want to sign up to get the discount, you go to www.lootcrate.com/criticalrole. if you want to sign up for the DX box, for the real collectors out there, that is www.lootcrate.com/criticalroledx.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is there a link in the chat or something, so that--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There is a link in the chat.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We'll also tweet one out or something.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we'll tweet at the break.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so I'm going to keep this. And you're not going to tell anyone.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Are we supposed to give it back? 'Cause that's not happening.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Well, they might belong to Geek and Sundry, but.

payload.raw_text: Yeah, yeah. We're going to be there. Looks like we may have a couple panels throughout the week, as well as the main Geek and Sundry panel, so if you guys are planning on coming to that area, keep an eye on the @CriticalRole Twitter, we're going to be announcing those times and rooms when they become available to us, so you can plan to come see us because we'd love to see you out there.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: You guys won't tell anyone, right? Anyway, on to the rest of the announcements! Next up, at E3, which is next week, we're going to be showing up on Thursday as part of GameSpot's E3 coverage. We're going to be doing cycling interviews with them starting at noon on Thursday, I believe, next week. So check in with GameSpot Thursday next week to have us sit in front of a camera and make complete and utter fools of ourselves, unlike we do here every Thursday. So that'll be a lot of fun. A reminder, me and Marisha are going to be at the Haven Expo convention in Australia beginning of July, so all you Australian folks in the Southern Hemisphere want to come and say hi, we'd love to see you. Comic Con, San Diego Comic Con is coming up.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I've heard of that.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It is quite a lot of gold. As you start counting up a section of it, and you being a very, very discerning eye, you're looking upwards of around 78,000 gold pieces.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh man! Come on 20!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Also, I believe I was first to the pile. I was looking for what weapons there are and I also want to watch her, very closely, with everything she does.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Does this have to do with dragons? Is it an investigation about dragons?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Uh, no, this is more to do with the gold--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What about because I'm in a mountain and it's my favored terrain?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Your favored terrain, ironically, is not gold, it is mountains. However, when you level up as a ranger, if you choose to, your next favored terrain should be gold. Just as a character choice--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm just checking for-- I wanna be sure I'm rolling what I'm supposed to--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Ducktales! Woo-ooo!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: 13 is all that I rolled, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: With a swimming speed of 40 in gold.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So the armored gnome, Pike, leaps onto the pile of gold coins and eagerly sifts through with her giant ogre-ish hands. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It finds things with magical essences.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Scanlan, she's metaphorically handing you the Quall.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Here, do you want the Hunk of Quall? Do you want to hang onto it?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure, I'll Quall it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, make an arcana check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ooh! 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It sparks for a second, then goes dark again. Looking at it, it seems like whatever it was designed to find, it has found and expended most of its temporary enchantment.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Lame.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Boo.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So it doesn't do anything. So then I'll scan through the stuff, looking for magical dope items--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are there any pretty paintings that grab our attention?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There are some very pretty paintings.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're not taking any paintings.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I could roll it up, Grog, it's not your call!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: They're very heavy.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I got a 14, anyone want to help me look for magical scheisse?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'll help, I'll help you look!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You never know. You do also rummage through. You find another unbroken-- the glass itself is cracked a little bit, but it's still intact-- what appears to be a small brown, silver and gray layered liquid. It's like three different layers that are all sediment and separate from each other, based on how it's been laying there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A lot of things that were seemingly recently used in the recovery of the large entity. However, there is one unbroken vial that you see that contains a cloudy grey oil that swishes around on the inside; some of it tends to seep through the glass, almost, and then evaporate instantaneously on touching the air.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh! Who wants this creepy looking shit?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll take it! Can I? Have I seen anything like this before?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You haven't, actually.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Dammit. Can I sniff it to get any clues?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You sniff it and it smells metallic, strangely, as you pull the stopper off. It's not liquid, it's more like a thick oil. It's very viscous and slowly swishes in place as you rotate the glass.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I bet Gilmore would know what that was.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll hang onto this for now. It might be massage oil.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You never know.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You get to the point where you guys, late into the night, have carved away roughly 40 functional pounds of hide before you're exhausted. You're exhausted and there's still plenty of hide to pull off of, but it would take you a week and you can notice the more you go, the scent is changing. It's almost like the acidic contents of the body are beginning to accelerate decomposition. It is actually, essentially, now that the body is no longer living, the acidic bile that it usually utilizes as a weapon is slowly consuming its remains.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was thinking perhaps Keyleth might actually--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's true! You do have a people to rule. Both of you do.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, Keyleth, perhaps, might want to take this book. I am not leading anyone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you, Scanlan. Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth, you wanna trade the book for this stupid vase?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's not Secret Santa, we're not trading things. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, I guess because I happen to be holding the vase at the time, sure. No you can-- you can have it

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I can have the book?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, you can have the vase.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Wait, what's the book? The book is Leadership and what?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Leadership and Influence. If you guys want to take the evening to kind of look over these items, you could probably discern most of the nature of them.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, well, let's do that. Let's take a look at them overnight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to continue eviscerating a dragon.

payload.raw_text: Okay. All right. You all wake up. Breakfast is at your disposal. You begin to eat comfortably, you wash up, Vex--

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: Don't you mean, that's fowl? (groaning)

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Oh! Fowl!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: I don't even care. Trinket loves it.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: And Trinket is all over that. Trinket is now just lapping it up from the inside of the bed--

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yeah. I just go to sleep and I'll take a shower in the morning.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I would say, for the purposes of the travel, because you guys had a few days' travel from the actual Sphinx's lair, towards Westruun when you were travellng back, you had the opportunity to use Greater Restoration to get rid of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It takes six days of reading to take effect.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit. So if you read for two days and then pass it off to someone else, does it just restart?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's once a century it works.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit! Once a century?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I have a similar book, that I used--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What did you use it on?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you live for many centuries, Keyleth. You can have it nexties! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's actually a very fair point.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's kind of perfect.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was about to say, would you like a Manual of Quickness? Here, for you-- (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, for the next century?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So 99 years from now--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sure, I'll hold onto it for that long.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: When we're all cold and dead!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was holding onto it in the hopes of finding somebody long-lived to sell it to.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Speaking of old, are some of us older than we used to be?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are we still old? It would have worn off, right?

payload.raw_text: So, as you're walking up from the city of Westruun, carrying behind you the group of eager-to-leave refugees that have gathered around you, you notice the burning spires, and you see the remnants of the horde leaving westward behind the group of heroes that you all had feared had gone to the top of the mountain to meet their doom. They stand before you alive and resting, and as you approach to hail them, one of them, the half-elf female, hails you.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Very important cannonball competition we had to get to.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns_last5.text: I'm sorry that I was laughing loud enough for the mics to pick it up. Some people on Twitter were saying that they heard me laugh.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: That's fine. Patrick Rothfuss, joining us today!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Fabulous writer. Good person.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns_last5.text: That's not me.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Excuse me for just a minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's true, this one has been plundered.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This is no less or more. There's a risk either way. It's just the risk of running or the risk of staying.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Also the dragons know that the last dragon who came here died.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think the dragons' motives have probably shifted by now, and where the dragons might not be interested in plundering anymore, instead might be interested in any city that we are in.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Which, I imagine we're leaving soon, anyway.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, they might not believe we'd be here, but they also might try to--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Kerr, are you an honest man?

preceding_turns.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns.text: I've been called an honest man.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I insight check him!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can I insight check-- Oh yeah, you do that. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You may both make insight checks.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (high voice) Four.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: If you whisper to Grog, it's going to make me really happy. Yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He just said, you fart.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Mightily.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, it's been almost two days since the initial encounter.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: He was real brave.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: There's only about 200 people left. Do you know someone named Amelia?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You know of Amelia, though you-- No, you did see her last night. You hadn't seen her in a while. She was one of the people that was still in the town. She was peripheral to the battle with the dragon, but fled as soon as she saw it. Unfortunately, dragons have that effect on people. She was a little shamed by it, but you calmed her down. But you do know that she's safe and reachable.

preceding_turns.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, she's around.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Where is everyone staying within the town? Are they scattered about or are they staying in a central location?

preceding_turns.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns.text: We've organized places for people to sleep if they don't have places for themselves. It's makeshift, but-- because it's been a couple of days, at this point?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. You guys make your way towards town. The largest concentration of survivors that you've been keeping tabs on actually aren't that far from Wilhand's home, in the area where most of the temples are constructed, which is generally considered one of the oldest and most protected areas of Westruun. There are two inns that are basically now ramshackle homes for sleep. There's bedspreads across the entire floor, all the tables and chairs have been moved out, and it's now just a giant room where people who had their homes destroyed come and sleep, not because it's comfortable, but because there's comfort and safety in numbers. People have gathered what belongings they still had that are worth anything or they might need for a journey. Many of them are still gathering bits of dried food and fruit and whatever, preparing for whatever the next step of their travels may be. But you probably have the bulk of the people in the town, other than a few older, very stubborn folks that are like, "I'm going to stay in my home!" The folks that stayed at Mt. St. Helens, where you're like, come on, guys, seriously? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's been almost two days since the initial encounter.

preceding_turns.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns.text: Right, so we've got triage camps set up. I will say, some people are going to stay no matter what, and they have the right to stay, and that's their choice. I imagine some people will come back and rebuild here, but most people, they are ready to go. They miss their families.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: An entire city.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We can build a dragon shelter. At the very least, that is something we can do.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But these people are prisoners here. They feel like they're prisoners in their own home, and that's not a home.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Then we let them make their choice.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: They'll do what they're going to do, but we can give them an option if things get hairy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's make our way toward them, then.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You see her. She's asleep. It's later in the day from the travel, you guys went down the mountain to even get here after you woke up in the morning. You spent most of the day getting back to Westruun, so you're getting pretty close to sunset. The group has been set off for the evening's travel toward the top of Gatshadow. She appears to have fallen asleep early.

preceding_turns.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns.text: There's always some.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There's always some. But yeah, so as you approach and the door swings open to the first tavern, as you introduce them too, a few of the folks perk up. A few children who couldn't sleep look over. Two of the kids that recognize you run up, "Uncle Kerrek!" They run up and grab the side of your legs. Your chain mail clanks and you pick them up.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I look around for Wilhand.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Wilhand is not amongst this group. Wilhand probably stayed safe within his home, which kept him safe up to this point, so far.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do we see Amelia?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Looking about, yes, you do see Amelia. She is asleep right now, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wake her up! Tell her her dad's dead!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hey, hey. It's really important. I need to tell you right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: We see her?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You see her. She's asleep. It's later in the day from the travel, you guys went down the mountain to even get here after you woke up in the morning. You spent most of the day getting back to Westruun, so you're getting pretty close to sunset. The group has been set off for the evening's travel toward the top of Gatshadow. She appears to have fallen asleep early.

preceding_turns.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns.text: There's always some.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There's always some. But yeah, so as you approach and the door swings open to the first tavern, as you introduce them too, a few of the folks perk up. A few children who couldn't sleep look over. Two of the kids that recognize you run up, "Uncle Kerrek!" They run up and grab the side of your legs. Your chain mail clanks and you pick them up.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I look around for Wilhand.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Wilhand is not amongst this group. Wilhand probably stayed safe within his home, which kept him safe up to this point, so far.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do we see Amelia?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Looking about, yes, you do see Amelia. She is asleep right now, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wake her up! Tell her her dad's dead!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hey, hey. It's really important. I need to tell you right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: We see her?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 32? All right. You look about the sky. It is clear. The dark clouds that had loomed towards the top of Gatshadow have since blown away. The starry night sky now spreads infinitely above you, and for the first time in a long time, knowing that the immediate danger is no longer chasing you, you take a minute to appreciate the sheer beauty of the view that Westruun always had. This brief moment to allow yourself to exhale helps remind you what all this is worth, and the importance of moments like this. You can hear the laughter behind you within the inn. You can hear the music. You can hear the kids chuckling and telling jokes. People clinking glasses together. And in this tiny little microcosm of a moment here, you let yourself silently smile, and really know what all of this is worth.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's medieval times, everybody drinks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Without much worry at that point, everyone's getting nice and liquored up, and happiness has ensued. Some folks pull out some meager instruments. There aren't a lot of extremely practiced bards, so your ears tweak a little at the sound of imperfect music, but there is honesty and joy coming from it, so you choose not to lend any sort of constructive criticism to the performers. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You sent the last of the decent bards out of town. It's all on you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I hang out outside? I want to keep my eye on the skies.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, not a worry. Go ahead and make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was about to say I was going to join you. I've had my fill of party.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I'm sure you were.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, what is that? 32.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's okay.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Hi, yes! Thank you again for releasing me from the terrible stocks. I really appreciate that. Thank you for everything you've done for Westruun."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, no, that's-- I wasn't-- Yeah. Yeah. I tell Kern--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Kern?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Car. Kerr.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Somewhere in that neighborhood.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Care Bear. I tell Kerr, even surrounded by friends, I often feel so alone. Thank you for being a friend, even though we just met.

preceding_turns.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns.text: I get that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He brings you in. Gets you another drink.

preceding_turns.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns.text: Actually, I'll bring another drink out to her.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, perfect. All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's crowded inside.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I stumble outside for a bit as well at this point.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Meanwhile, I'm telling my table a story about how I won a cannonball contest last night.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They are enraptured.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It was in a dragon's lair! I was amazing. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is Amelia awake at this point?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Amelia has woken up due to the sound and noise, and she's wandering about with a drink in her hand now.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: All right. We'll walk over to her.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll stay with you, but just behind you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Amelia?

payload.raw_text: Welcome back. So as you return here, bellies full, the evening of drinks, rabble-rousing, and stress release through conversation and laughter carries through until the morning light comes. Everyone wakes up extremely hungover, but in a way that is well-earned and still worthy of the evening that transpired before it. You all come to consciousness, about midday at this time now.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: I pour my flagon into hers.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: She laughs and drinks of it again. Definitely embraced the idea that she's going to get really drunk tonight. She begins joining in, singing with the music. And the rest of the party picks up as the celebration once again fills the air. The rest of the folks gathering around. (birds tweeting) Don't know why there were birds in that track. (laughter)

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns_last5.text: Is it morning?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: There will be a lot of partying through the night, so probably it's an eventuality. However, we will take this moment to take a quick break to the restroom. We will return here for the next bit of this adventure, so we'll be right back here in a few minutes, guys. Stick around. (cheering) [break]

payload.raw_text: Welcome back. So as you return here, bellies full, the evening of drinks, rabble-rousing, and stress release through conversation and laughter carries through until the morning light comes. Everyone wakes up extremely hungover, but in a way that is well-earned and still worthy of the evening that transpired before it. You all come to consciousness, about midday at this time now.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: I pour my flagon into hers.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: She laughs and drinks of it again. Definitely embraced the idea that she's going to get really drunk tonight. She begins joining in, singing with the music. And the rest of the party picks up as the celebration once again fills the air. The rest of the folks gathering around. (birds tweeting) Don't know why there were birds in that track. (laughter)

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns_last5.text: Is it morning?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: There will be a lot of partying through the night, so probably it's an eventuality. However, we will take this moment to take a quick break to the restroom. We will return here for the next bit of this adventure, so we'll be right back here in a few minutes, guys. Stick around. (cheering) [break]

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: There's a parchment that's just nailed to the outside of the keep. At least it's good calligraphy. All right, so unless you have anything else to prepare in this time, most of the townsfolk are beginning to converge towards the square in the central-southern region of Westruun where you had the showdown battle with Kevdak outside of the Margrave's home. The now deceased Margrave. Everyone seems to have gathered together and there is an air of tension. As you approach, as folks are muttering to themselves, then it gets quiet as you all step into the central area. There is still-- it's been pulled over, it was partially damaged before, but there is a large elevated platform that was used for speeches or celebration elements that has been, for the time being, fixed to allow you guys to stand up if you wish to address the rest of the folks.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Your scry pie?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Glancing at it this moment now, and it's about midday, you concentrate on what you left behind.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yep. We all know what it was.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You have a brief view, a small windowed view of the kitchen before you. It seems to be currently empty.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But still standing?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But still standing. You do notice, in the sink there are some fresh dishes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh! Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Squatters.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's fucking squatters.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Or maybe our staff.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Unpaid now, so now they're squatters.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) It's an important distinction.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can you talk through your poop?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, no. Poo doesn't work that way, Travis. It's a one-way street.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The finer points are lost on me.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: You left it in the kitchen?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I left it in a jar in the kitchen and I told the people not to open it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We only got a C on our general inspection rating. (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: One gentleman takes the stage next to you guys, man in his younger 20s, kind of soot smeared on his cheeks. Looks like he's a little weathered by the experience. Even though he looks young and thin, there is a general hardness to his face from recent experiences. He kind of raises up, looking at the both of you, below him: "I, for one, miss my family and have no home to go back to. And the the homes that are empty in this town right now are empty because those who lived there are now dead. There are ghosts here I wish not to see every morning when I wake up. If I'm going to live here, I want to return with those that are important to me and build a new home, over the old. Now is not my time to stay. Especially if more of those things are coming back. If anyone wishes to come with me to Kymal, please, stand with me." And he steps off the stage and make his way to the right side of the stage. Everyone in the crowd looks to each other, and few folks begin filtering off and gathering around him. I'd say about a third to half of the crowd begins to shift over as another person steps up right next to you guys, actually. This is a woman in her late 40s or so. She's wearing relatively nice clothing that's been kind of torn apart through constant use and not really having an option to change into. She looks like she came from money, but has definitely been pulled down to the same level as the rest of the folks here; they're living in destitution out of necessity. Her hair's trying to be pulled up into a bun, but a lot of it is kind of falling around her face, and she's keeping this kind of poor facade of her previous life. And she stands up: "I myself, my family's history is here in this town and I would be remiss if I didn't express my hesitation in leaving that behind, for the next roving horde of barbarians to rush in and take from us while we're away. Especially if this gold is to be retrieved, how do we know how much of that is to be distributed? How much was my family's versus yours? If we're to divide ourselves at this point, we're leaving ourselves open to inner conflict, lest we have a very clear idea where these funds are going. And I, for one, want to make sure I have a home when my family returns. So I'm not leaving. I'm not going to come back here months down the line and find four layabouts living in that home, made a complete mess of our things, and telling us it's now theirs. Those who wish to stay, stand with me." (harrumphs) And she walks offstage, to the side--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (whispering and murmuring) Whispers through the crowds.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: As we speak, upwards of 40,000 gold are being delivered by your finest men. This, with our help, can help construct a sturdy underground layer that will shelter you if the dragon were to come back. It will take days, possibly weeks to construct this. But if you wanted to stay, this would be your best bet. However, we are not forcing you to do anything. If you would like to go with the fellow refugees to Kymal, or find your fellow citizens scattered about, roving in the lowlands, that would be fine too. We need to come to an agreement today, so that we can help you do what you want.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I'll come with you. I'd like to see him one last time. We have no family plot. We were meager folk. But he spent most of his life working in that field. I think it's only fitting that's where he spends the rest of his days. Show me." And as you guys begin the building process, she follows you out to the field where she is shown the body of Reginald, and she takes a moment of quiet with him, and cradles his body. But there's a smile on her face, and she says thank you for letting her say goodbye face to face. With your help, you both bring the body back to the farmhouse where you had first met, and you spend the rest of the day interring his body within the hillside that is buttressed against the actual farmland that he tilled.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that one. And the giant bag of gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I seek her out in the crowd.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She's still looking up, kind of by herself in the crowd, clapping off to the side as you approach.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to hang near him.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Excuse me, young miss.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes? Hi."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry. We haven't spoken, but you spoke to my friends. You spoke to my sister. My sister looks very much--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I can see the similarity, yes."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I know the news they delivered to you. I am so sorry for your loss. Your father was very brave at the end. Impressively so, and I want you to know that. I've also recovered his body, and I thought to add him to the pyres that this city did outside the city, but I thought maybe you had a family plot or somewhere you'd prefer him to be laid to rest. I'll gladly take him there for you, or take him with you. Whatever you prefer.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. Now. You guys begin the process of building this subterranean bunker. You take the first day, scanning through as a group, clearing out whatever creatures lurk beneath the city. Nothing of severe threat lies down there to your power level, and you know, what once was a threat to you guys long ago, within the period of a day, you've cleared out a very, very sizable portion of the sewer system. And because it's been empty for quite a while, it's also not in a very terrible state either. I mean, it still needs to be cleaned, but that's also the specialty of those who can control water and air, let alone reinforcing the stonework that keeps the walls themselves sturdy, strong, able to support any heavy weight on the surface. Within the next couple days, you guys continue the reinforcing process. People begin actually moving their supplies down underground and begin taking bits of furniture and elements that would at least make life down there more comfortable, or at least, a ghost image of some of the life they lived above. Because when you're down there, the comforts are what keep you going. But the people seem, as more and more of it comes together, folks genuinely begin to get a little excited at the idea of having a safe place to live for a while. And you feel like this is doing some good for these folks at the very least. Whenever you guys would like to take your journey--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: To lose a parent before their time is no easy thing. I'm very sorry for you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It's all right. He did good."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That he did.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Let's go ahead and make sure the rest of these folk have more to look forward to, shall we?" She looks at you for a second, and you can see she takes a general scope of your expression and your emotional state, and even for a young girl, she has a stronger insight than you expected, and she puts her arm out and touches your shoulder and says, "You carry too much sadness with you. You've done a lot of good. Let yourself-- Let yourself understand and accept the fact that you've done a lot of good." And she starts walking back towards the town.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I linger for a bit.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. Now. You guys begin the process of building this subterranean bunker. You take the first day, scanning through as a group, clearing out whatever creatures lurk beneath the city. Nothing of severe threat lies down there to your power level, and you know, what once was a threat to you guys long ago, within the period of a day, you've cleared out a very, very sizable portion of the sewer system. And because it's been empty for quite a while, it's also not in a very terrible state either. I mean, it still needs to be cleaned, but that's also the specialty of those who can control water and air, let alone reinforcing the stonework that keeps the walls themselves sturdy, strong, able to support any heavy weight on the surface. Within the next couple days, you guys continue the reinforcing process. People begin actually moving their supplies down underground and begin taking bits of furniture and elements that would at least make life down there more comfortable, or at least, a ghost image of some of the life they lived above. Because when you're down there, the comforts are what keep you going. But the people seem, as more and more of it comes together, folks genuinely begin to get a little excited at the idea of having a safe place to live for a while. And you feel like this is doing some good for these folks at the very least. Whenever you guys would like to take your journey--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: To lose a parent before their time is no easy thing. I'm very sorry for you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It's all right. He did good."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That he did.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Let's go ahead and make sure the rest of these folk have more to look forward to, shall we?" She looks at you for a second, and you can see she takes a general scope of your expression and your emotional state, and even for a young girl, she has a stronger insight than you expected, and she puts her arm out and touches your shoulder and says, "You carry too much sadness with you. You've done a lot of good. Let yourself-- Let yourself understand and accept the fact that you've done a lot of good." And she starts walking back towards the town.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I linger for a bit.

payload.raw_text: Yeah. I mean, there is disease, as tends to happen when you have people who are displaced and clustered like that in sub-par living conditions, but no major pandemics that are kicking through the people there. The people who are sick are being tended to, and there are people who are medically practiced, or at least herbalists and such, that can help with that. You find a slight group of trustworthy individuals that you know would do well and understand the process, and with the mere inclusion of a few thousand gold, would be more than enough to be able to purchase from the interior of Kymal enough oxen, horses, and other beasts to bring most of the current carts back with you. That was the only real barrier, because Kymal was like, "We can't help you guys for free." Now there's gold, people are like, "Oh! Well, let's talk." Charity only goes so far. But you do feel through this trip that you've definitely made an impact, and most of the people, at least over the next week or so, will be outfitted with the proper materials, food for the journey, and be able to make their way back towards Westruun.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: That's true.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: Either way.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns_last5.text: I know what's needed back there. I've got access to money. They have wagons and labor. I know most of the people in town, so I'm not going to do it myself, but I know who-- honestly, anyone who runs a farm is a better organizer than any modern small business owner.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: This is very true.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns_last5.text: So I will talk to a few of these old, capable farmer types and say, okay, here's the money. You know how to bargain. Do your business, and we can get together and we'll head back all together. As soon as you can get everyone together and all that. Also, I'll put somebody in charge of making sure that we're bringing in food, and I'll do a scan around and make sure that people literally aren't dying of dysentery. That they have a clean well and all of that stuff.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah. I mean, there is disease, as tends to happen when you have people who are displaced and clustered like that in sub-par living conditions, but no major pandemics that are kicking through the people there. The people who are sick are being tended to, and there are people who are medically practiced, or at least herbalists and such, that can help with that. You find a slight group of trustworthy individuals that you know would do well and understand the process, and with the mere inclusion of a few thousand gold, would be more than enough to be able to purchase from the interior of Kymal enough oxen, horses, and other beasts to bring most of the current carts back with you. That was the only real barrier, because Kymal was like, "We can't help you guys for free." Now there's gold, people are like, "Oh! Well, let's talk." Charity only goes so far. But you do feel through this trip that you've definitely made an impact, and most of the people, at least over the next week or so, will be outfitted with the proper materials, food for the journey, and be able to make their way back towards Westruun.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's true.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Either way.

preceding_turns.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns.text: I know what's needed back there. I've got access to money. They have wagons and labor. I know most of the people in town, so I'm not going to do it myself, but I know who-- honestly, anyone who runs a farm is a better organizer than any modern small business owner.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This is very true.

preceding_turns.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns.text: So I will talk to a few of these old, capable farmer types and say, okay, here's the money. You know how to bargain. Do your business, and we can get together and we'll head back all together. As soon as you can get everyone together and all that. Also, I'll put somebody in charge of making sure that we're bringing in food, and I'll do a scan around and make sure that people literally aren't dying of dysentery. That they have a clean well and all of that stuff.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I don't know, you've done quite a bit of work in even these past few weeks. Vasselheim. I'll talk it over with the doctor. See what he's interested in. You promised."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, you do the same, all right? Stay safe. I got you a present. It's a ring. It's a beautiful ring because you're a beautiful girl, and I'd like you to keep it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She takes it and looks at it and goes, "Are you sure this wasn't for someone else?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No. Never.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She puts it on.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And you're also strong, and you'll need this sword. And I give her my Singing Dawn Blade, which has a unique little imperfection at the bottom of it, and I say, this sword has killed a king, which makes it almost worthy enough for you. I hope that it keeps you safe until I can see you again.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't know what to say. I'll start with thank you. Well, I feel pretty well protected."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Get out of here. If you can, try to make your way to Vasselheim. There's three more dragons, and it'll be a while before I kill them all.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Thank you." They keep walking. So, with the money dispersed, the group having a returning sense of hope, with materials, goods, food being redistributed amongst the refugees, it appears there is a direction for the future of Westruun that you've set into place. As you guys gather the rest of your things, Keyleth, you take an evening's rest with the troupe, and the next morning return back to Westruun.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Before they go, I just reach out my hand and introduce myself to Kaylie. I've heard many lovely things about you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She looks at you guardedly and takes your hand. "As I've heard of you. He made a promise."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'll make sure that he keeps it.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. As you guys spend the next few days together, forming what is a fairly functional stronghold, for at least the necessary needs of protecting a fair amount of refugees beneath the northern area of Westruun, you feel confident that with the folks that are here and the folks that are coming, there's enough of a community building now where it may not be absolutely necessary for your perpetual oversight and presence, should you have business elsewhere. Anything else you wish to take care of before you continue your journey?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm just reading these names on this wall!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What's your passive perception?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You hear a few very, very faint, very familiar steps come across the stones behind you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I pretend like I don't hear them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Stubby! And I pull her into a big fat hug. Your hair looks like shit, sit down. I start braiding it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I love you too.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Elaina would be proud of you. I'm proud of you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's worth more.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Saved my life again.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Did I?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I crawled out through an arrow hole.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) I saw this little lump squirming. I thought it was probably you. Tiny little you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: There's a bit of chicken here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, ow!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: One day at a time, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: One day at a time. Ow!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: There.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You're going on the better part of two weeks since the initial dragon attack. A little over two weeks, maybe, by now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I have a small package and letter that I want to deliver to Kerr before we leave, but other than that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: A Kerr package?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes. I was hoping, I was really hoping, someone would say that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I would like to pull Kerr aside and say, you know these people here, they're a bit lost, and they need a leader.

preceding_turns.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, they absolutely do. We'll see if we can find them one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm looking at one that would be wonderful. Just something to think about.

preceding_turns.speaker: PATRICK ROTHFUSS
preceding_turns.text: I've thought about it. But no. But you're very kind. You know, over-- How long has this been going on?

payload.raw_text: But yes, as you step back to the surface beyond the subterranean personnel bunker that is now the sewage system of Westruun, you find yourself at a nearby tree. The doorway opens, and you all dart through into the cold, snow-dappled, cobbled streets of Vasselheim. And that's where we'll end tonight's game. Picking up next week, at that moment. Thank you guys so much, that was a great session! Dude, Patrick, thank you so much for joining us!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: To outside a building in Vasselheim.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Indeed.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: To a far wooded area where magic is permissible, outside of Vasselheim.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Divine magic is permissible. The essence of pulling magic beyond the powers. It's the arcane practitioning that is frowned upon. It's not illegal. It's just generally frowned upon.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: It's rude.
